observed: Initial project standards created for baseline, autonomy, environment, and runbook.
rule_added: Read AGENTS, VERSTKA_STANDARD, NEURO_VERSTKA_PREP, FIGMA_AUTONOMY_PROTOCOL before any task.

observed: Root canvas extraction hit Figma MCP quota after initial metadata pass.
rule_added: For full-file Figma work, capture a manifest first, then extract per-screen context and screenshots, and when MCP quota is exhausted continue from local export bundles instead of stopping the task.

observed: Local export bundle contains enough HTML and asset data to produce a useful screen manifest without Figma MCP.
rule_added: When Figma MCP quota blocks deeper extraction, generate a local manifest from the export bundle and _unzipped HTML, then use that manifest as the implementation source of truth for the remaining screens.

observed: Local manifest, screen-specs, implementation queue, and code implementation plan all resolve to the current baseline with zero missing screens.
rule_added: If implementation queue and code plan report zero drift, treat the baseline as validated and only record follow-up diffs when a future export changes.

observed: Export frame for screen 02 is 1200x1000 and the testimonial copy must wrap into exactly five lines at desktop width while the section keeps the export frame rhythm.
rule_added: When a Figma screen export includes a fixed frame height, align the HTML section min-height to that frame and tune copy width until the export line breaks match exactly.

observed: The Nebula workspace can run a fully local autonomous learning loop using only _unzipped, the export bundle, manifest/spec generation, and local verification, while Telegram can consume the resulting report as the operator UI.
rule_added: Treat local manifest/spec/queue/plan generation as the primary learning polygon; record only evidence-backed reusable rules, and keep Codex out of the loop when local exports already answer the question.

observed: A file-based Telegram bridge is enough for Nebula to expose autonomous learning status without building a new backend runtime.
rule_added: When a Telegram UI already exists, let it read the latest local payload file instead of duplicating learning logic in the bot process.

observed: The Figma web file is reachable in a live browser session even when Figma MCP is quota-limited, and the export bundle can be normalized into a single figma-export package root.
rule_added: Use the live browser session for file metadata and layer inspection when MCP is blocked, then package outputs under H:\Nebula\GPT\figma-export\<timestamp>\ with a normalized builder script.

observed: The browser-backed export package is now validated end-to-end with required root-level browser-capture.json, browser-layer-index.json, screenshots, and the local manifest/spec bundle.
rule_added: Require browser-capture.json at the package root and keep screenshots as direct review artifacts in screenshots/ for the latest live-browser package.

observed: A small publishable node in Figma can be exported natively through the browser UI by using Copy as PNG/SVG, reading the system clipboard, and saving the bytes/text directly into H:\Nebula\GPT\figma-export\<timestamp>\exports.
rule_added: For native browser export, prefer small publishable nodes first, use Copy as PNG for bitmap and Copy as SVG for vector, then save clipboard payloads directly into exports/; if a large selection trips the clipboard limit, fall back to SVG or a smaller node.

observed: The export workflow now has a repeatable node-by-node plan derived from the live browser layer index, and the manifest records the latest browser export package as first-class metadata.
rule_added: When a browser-backed Figma export is in play, generate a node-by-node export plan from the layer index, keep browser-native exports in exports/, and rebuild the package after every manifest or export-plan change.

observed: The export workflow now has a file-driven batch runner that turns the live browser layer index into 39 node-level jobs and a batch summary package artifact.
rule_added: Treat batch-summary.json and job files as first-class export artifacts, and require package validation after every change to the manifest, export plan, or runner.
